title: Saccoloma distans
taxonomy: Saccoloma distans is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is commonly found in humid, shaded environments. The classification of Saccoloma distans places it within the genus Saccoloma, which is characterized by its unique leaf morphology and distribution of sori. Saccoloma distans is further classified as a species of vascular plant, specifically a type of fern within the division Pteridophyta. The classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other fern species.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Saccoloma distans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
